     The Numbers Business Report

Saving Mr. Banks (2013)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $83,299,761Details
International Box Office $31,662,764
Worldwide Box Office $114,962,525
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $20,228,975
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $8,644,483
Total $28,873,458
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In 1961, Walt Disney invited “Mary Poppins” author P.L. Travers to his studio in Los Angeles to discuss, in person, his continued interest in obtaining the movie rights to her beloved book and character—a pitch he first made to her in the 1940s. Still hesitant and disinterested after all those years, Travers wanted to tell the Hollywood impresario to go fly a kite but with dwindling sales of her books and a bleak economic future looming, P.L. Travers said yes and embarked on a two-week sojourn in Los Angeles that would ultimately set the wheels of the beloved film in motion.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$413,373 (0.5% of total gross)
Legs:6.19 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:72.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$35,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:15 opening theaters/2,671 max. theaters, 5.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $122,259,413

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 13th, 2013 (Limited) by Walt Disney
December 20th, 2013 (Expands Wide) by Walt Disney
International Releases: November 29th, 2013 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
December 12th, 2013 (Wide) (Netherlands)
January 31st, 2014 (Wide), released as Ao Encontro de Mr. Banks (Portugal)
Video Release: March 18th, 2014 by Buena Vista Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for thematic elements including some unsettling images.
(Rating bulletin 2267, 4/10/2013)
Running Time: 125 minutes
Plot point: Biography, Dysfunctional Family, Non-Chronological, Writing and Writers
Social setting: Movie Business
Subgenre: Historical Drama
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Ruby Films, Essential Media and Entertainment, BBC Films, Hopscotch Features
Production Countries: United States, United Kingdom
Languages: English

Synopsis

In 1961, Walt Disney invited “Mary Poppins” author P.L. Travers to his studio in Los Angeles to discuss, in person, his continued interest in obtaining the movie rights to her beloved book and character—a pitch he first made to her in the 1940s. Still hesitant and disinterested after all those years, Travers wanted to tell the Hollywood impresario to go fly a kite but with dwindling sales of her books and a bleak economic future looming, P.L. Travers said yes and embarked on a two-week sojourn in Los Angeles that would ultimately set the wheels of the beloved film in motion.
